text: Same Day Laparoscopic Cholecystectomy Pathway: e Patient to be scheduled 1%, 2" on the AM list. e Patient to be fit with no or minimal comorbidities. o General guide to exclusion criteria to be considered: = NoASA3orabove = BMI|>/=35 = OSANOTon CPAP = Chronic pain = Multiple allergies to pain medication * Must be independent with ADLs. = Language barriers/CALD = Surgical exclusions: difficulty, adhesions bleeding. e Patient must have a good social support network to care for them post operatively at home upon discharge. e On DOS patient will need to meet all relevant discharge criteria as per the AKMR20. e Postoperative scripts will be provided to the patient for pain and nausea following the suggested medications. Post op analgesia "protocol": Regular analgesia I e Paracetamol 1gq.i.d. po e Anti-inflammatory of choice (Celecoxib 100mg bd po) PRN analgesia e Tramadol IR 50mg po q6h OR Tapentadol IR 50mg po q6h x10tabs e OR Buprenorphine 200mcg g4h sublingual x10 tabs (only if doesn't tolerate tramadol). Antiemetic ¢ Ondansetron 4mg po q6h x5 tabs ¢ Patient will be provided with an instruction sheet as attached (draft- still for feedback) ¢ Patient to be reassured and provided education on discharge in relation to the medications prescribed and advised if they have any concerns, they have the contact number of the After-hours CNS. Patient to be advised to expect a follow up phone call the next day. e APS Nurse- to contact patient Day 1 after discharge for follow up: o Check pain o Nausea and vomiting o Anyother concerns * If any concerns noted from the APS nurse to be escalated to the treating consultant via team registrar, = APS Nurse- to document follow up in APS nurse led clinic documents.
